artifact: Capability Ă— Importance Ă— Coverage matrix (Lens 3 â€” business value)
purpose: Make 'where is high business value weakly covered' legible at a glance, across all three test surfaces (human UI Â· AI-agent/MCP Â· automated-test). The Lens-3 'where to invest' artifact the next-gen testing program was missing.
kind: periodic â€” refresh on any surface change (new MCP tool, new test, new module). NOT a per-run gate.
verifiedAgainst: origin/main @ f0c7b426 + live mast-mcp tool surface (read-only, golden-auric/sgtest15) on 2026-06-22
provenanceNote: Coverage claims are LIVE-verified, not copied from the 2026-06-20 scorecard. The re-verification surfaced positive drift: mast_procurement (W4) and mast_production (W2) now exist â€” the scorecard called them 'UI-only / no tool'. See coverageRefresh.

sources.moduleCatalog: Sell Â· Make Â· Ship Â· Market Â· Show Â· Run Â· Web Â· Book Â· Manage (the 9-module nav/gamification catalog)
sources.agentCoverage: live mast-mcp registry + read calls (golden-auric) 2026-06-22; suite-scorecard.json agent.coverage as prior baseline
sources.testCoverage: test/ (35 *.test.js) + 20 scripts/lint-*.js + test/money-canary.test.js + scripts/smoke-boot.mjs (67-route fail-closed Playwright)
sources.importanceAnchors: testing-program-discovery.md Â§5E â€” JTBD/ODI (invest where importance high & satisfaction low), RICE/opportunity scoring, outcomes-over-outputs; suite-scorecard.json moneyCritical flag

importanceRubric.note: RICE-lite, value-weighted. Three transparent signals, money weighted 2x. Composite drives the tier; tiers are defensible orderings, not false precision.

money.3: moves money / restates the books / compliance (moneyCritical:true)

revenueLine.2: a whole revenue line or sales channel

agent.gap: wrong/thin collection â€” value metric absent

test.proven: behavioral/value regression net (assertions on real math)
test.structural: boot-smoke route render only â€” no behavioral assertion

capabilities.jtbd: When I've made a new item, I want it priced and live on my storefront so I can sell it.

importance.rationale: Weekly; sets price/margin & makes the catalog sellable (revenue-enabling).

ui.note: materials â†’ recipe builder â†’ product editor â†’ publish gate â†’ storefront

agent.note: mast_products create/get + mast_recipes + mast_materials + materialize_variants + set_status

test.note: product-readiness.test.js, variant-reconcile.test.js, lint-readiness-parity (SHA-pinned UIâ†”MCP gate); no create round-trip E2E

capabilities.jtbd: When I sell at the counter or a fair, I want it recorded so inventory and revenue are right.

importance.rationale: Daily/seasonal; money-critical revenue accuracy â€” the cents/Ă—100/double-count hot path.

ui.note: #pos sales ledger â†’ separate-tab register â†’ receipt â†’ inventory + finance

agent.note: UI-only for creation by design; the agent is the ORACLE (finance_get_revenue byChannel + mast_products inventory before/after)

test.note: money-canary.test.js (POS double-count, cents-vs-dollars, channel canonicality), finance-pos-dedup.test.js, pos-tax-jurisdiction.test.js, orders-core.test.js

capabilities.capability: Online storefront purchase (cart â†’ checkout â†’ order)
capabilities.jtbd: When a customer orders online, the sale and order must be captured correctly.

importance.rationale: Daily core revenue; the storefront buy flow that feeds fulfillment + finance.

ui.note: storefront checkout.js â†’ order created

agent.note: creation is storefront-gated by design; agent uses mast_orders create_test on test tenants; order reads are protected (F2 handshake)

test.note: orders-core.test.js, finance-order-money.test.js; no cartâ†’checkout E2E in-repo

capabilities.jtbd: When I work with a customer (esp. wholesale), I want their full picture and the right pricing.

importance.rationale: Daily; retention/AOV (not directly money-moving â€” moneyCritical:false).

ui.note: customers-v2 (V2 lens over V1 CustomersBridge): LTV, sorts (spend/orders/lapse/gross-margin-12m), Retail/Wholesale filters, saved segments

agent.note: F9 (LIVE-CONFIRMED 2026-06-22): no mast_customers tool. mast_contacts is a DIFFERENT thin collection (name/email/phone/category only; 50 records on golden-auric) â€” no LTV / wholesale tier / segment / spend / lapse. The customer-value surface is UI-only.

test.note: customer-filters.test.js (D4-005 over-inclusion class), customer-resolver.test.js (email dedup), lint-customer-writes

capabilities.jtbd: When I build a recipe, I want accurate unit cost so my price protects margin.

importance.rationale: Weekly; the costing engine underpins every price (margin).

ui.note: recipe builder exists; the UI exposes less of the cost engine than the MCP

agent.note: mast_recipes get/recalculate/get_cost_basis_diff/set_active_tier/get_margin_history â€” exceeds the UI

test.note: mast-entity.test.js (engine); the cost engine itself is MCP-side, not unit-pinned in this repo

capabilities.jtbd: When my material price moves, I want to reprice affected products before margin erodes.

importance.rationale: As markets move; margin-protection (money-critical), but supporting not a revenue line.

ui.note: the UI exposes less of this than the agent

agent.note: mast_recipes list_needing_reprice / get_drift / simulate_metal_shift (zero-write) / reprice; mast_price_locks to verify locked items skipped â€” notably stronger than UI

test.note: no reprice/drift regression in this repo (recipe engine lives MCP-side); UI surface is partial AND untested

capabilities.jtbd: When an order needs making, I want a build job so it gets produced and tracked.

importance.rationale: Weekly; WIP/cost + enables fulfillment of made-to-order revenue.

ui.note: jobs / production.js â€” create job, line items, builds

agent.note: UPGRADE (live 2026-06-22): mast_production list_jobs/create_job/add_line_item/update_line_item/list_requests/assign_request/list_operators â€” returned 3 real jobs on golden-auric. The 2026-06-20 scorecard's 'no production/pack MCP tools' is STALE for production.

test.note: jobs-transitions.test.js, workflow-overrides.test.js (MastFlow process transitions)

capabilities.capability: Fulfill order â†’ pack â†’ ship â†’ delivered
capabilities.jtbd: When a customer orders, I want to fulfill and ship it so they get their goods and I get paid.

importance.rationale: Daily; core revenue recognition + CX; historic bug hotspot (reserve/release field-path).

ui.note: orders-v2 triage â†’ production â†’ fulfillment.js (pack/ship/tracking) â†’ complete

agent.note: triage/status via mast_orders (protected, F2); production now covered (mast_production); but PACK/SHIP has no MCP tool â€” the agent cannot close the loop to 'delivered'

test.note: orders-core.test.js, jobs-transitions.test.js; no packâ†’ship E2E

capabilities.jtbd: When stock runs low, I want to reorder from my vendor so I don't run out.

importance.rationale: Weekly; continuity + cost control (moneyCritical:true); cost-basis write.

ui.note: reorder-v2 â†’ procurement-v2 (PO as MastFlow process) â†’ vendors-v2 â†’ receive â†’ inventory/cost-basis

agent.note: UPGRADE (live 2026-06-22): mast_procurement list/create_purchase_order/update/set_status/send_to_vendor/RECEIVE (creates lots + CF restocks on-hand) + mast_vendors + materials_cost_history. Tool works live (empty PO list on golden-auric, not error). The 2026-06-20 scorecard's 'PO write UI-only (no clear tool)' is STALE â€” full PO write loop now agent-operable.

test.note: NO procurement test; boot-smoke loads the procurement-v2 route only (structural). The POâ†’receiveâ†’restockâ†’cost-basis money path has no regression net despite new agent write power.

capabilities.jtbd: When a customer returns an item, I want to process it and refund them correctly.

importance.rationale: Weekly; money-OUT + CX; double/over-refund adversarial risk.

ui.note: rma â†’ return.workflow (MastFlow) â†’ refund (transitionRma CF) â†’ inventory/finance

agent.note: mast_cs_tickets read + mast_orders status (protected); refund itself is a server-side CF â€” agent observes, may not drive

test.note: no RMA/refund test; the over/double-refund collateral check the spine flagged (AppWorld-style) is unbuilt

capabilities.jtbd: When I want to drive demand, I want to author and send campaigns.

importance.rationale: Weekly; demand-gen (indirect revenue).

ui.note: campaigns/newsletter/social authoring UI

agent.note: MCP sends only transactional email (mast_email); no campaign/newsletter authoring tool

test.note: creative-quality is an LLM-as-judge/human + visual problem, NOT task-success â€” deliberately excluded from the dual spine

capabilities.jtbd: When I exhibit at or organize a market, I want to manage shows, booths and vendors.

importance.rationale: Seasonal; a sales channel + booth/vendor fees.

ui.note: events module â€” shows, booths, vendors, submissions, announcements, scavenger hunt

agent.note: mast_events full CRUD EXISTS (shows/booths/vendors) but is a PROTECTED action â€” even list_shows triggers the confirmation handshake (agent friction, F2-class). NB: this is the SHOW module (markets you exhibit at/organize), NOT the Book module (classes) â€” they are distinct.

test.note: no events test

capabilities.jtbd: When I need to know how the business is doing (or file taxes), I want accurate financials.

importance.rationale: Weekly + period-end; decisions + compliance (money-critical).

ui.note: finance-v2 hubs (revenue, P&L, AR/AP, tax, period-close)

agent.note: 10 finance read tools, token-cheap, self-reconciling (W6 matrix passes 6/6 live); F14 marginReliable/cogsLineMissingCount now present live (verified golden-auric: marginReliable:false, missing 10)

test.note: money-canary.test.js â€” THE cross-surface value assertion (revenue/byChannel/P&L/F14-margin-gate/F5-tax/AR aging vs golden 'Auric & Oak' fixtures, to the cent); finance-order-money, finance-pos-dedup

capabilities.jtbd: When the pay period closes, I want hours, PTO and labor cost right so I can pay my team.

importance.rationale: Pay-period; people + cost (money-critical).

ui.note: team.js â€” timeclock, PTO, payroll, commissions (team-v2/employees-v2)

agent.note: team_clock_in/out, team_record_pto, team_set_pto_policy, team_get_labor_cost, team_get_time_entries â€” fine-grained

test.note: lint-labor-parity SHA-pins the labor core UIâ†”MCP byte-identical; no labor unit test in-repo

capabilities.jtbd: When money goes out, I want it categorized so my books and taxes are right.

importance.rationale: Weekly; books/compliance/tax (money-critical).

ui.note: expenses hubs + categorization

agent.note: expenses_create/update/delete/categorize + finance_get_expenses + finance_get_1099_prep

test.note: money-canary.test.js exercises opexâ†’P&L routing; no expenses-specific regression

capabilities.jtbd: When I set up my store, I want a storefront and brand that sells.

importance.rationale: Set-and-maintain; IS the online revenue front-of-house (a whole revenue surface).

ui.note: website/storefront builder, brand, lookbooks, sections, templates

agent.note: UI-only builder; mast_domains/mast_hosting are infra, not the builder

test.note: boot-smoke renders the storefront routes only; theme-sanity.html is an unreferenced rotting copy. Visual/aesthetic â€” the right instrument is visual-regression + human review, deliberately outside the dual spine.

capabilities.jtbd: When I teach classes, I want to schedule them and enroll students so I can sell my time.

importance.rationale: Seasonal/weekly; a WHOLE second revenue line (money-critical).

ui.note: classes-v2 + book.js â€” an 11-route sub-app: enrollments, instructors, passes, sessions, resources, calendar

agent.note: F11 (LIVE-CONFIRMED 2026-06-22): NO classes/booking/enrollment/sessions/passes tool. mast_events=shows/booths, mast_missions=QA scenarios, trips=mileage â€” none cover this domain. The agent cannot operate this revenue line AT ALL.

test.note: boot-smoke renders the classes-v2/book routes only; zero behavioral/booking test â€” the largest untested domain.

capabilities.jtbd: When my catalog changes, I want products and stock accurate so I don't oversell or misprice.

importance.rationale: Daily; stock accuracy â†’ oversell/cost; lifecycle integrity.

ui.note: product record/variants/lifecycle; inventory adjustments; lots

agent.note: mast_products lifecycle + update_inventory + materialize_variants + set_status; mast_procurement lots

test.note: variant-reconcile.test.js, product-readiness.test.js; money-canary touches inventory-adjacent invariants

capabilities.jtbd: When I sign up, I want my store, channels and payment rails connected so I can transact.

importance.rationale: One-time; activation + payment rails (enabling).

ui.note: setup wizard, channel/payment connect, domains, hosting

agent.note: mast_provisioning, mast_import, mast_migration, mast_domains, mast_hosting exist; OAuth/secret-vault connect is one-time + external and can't be live-verified on sgtest15

test.note: wizard-launch-never-blocks.test.js pins the wizard-launch gate; channel/payment connect untested (external deps)

capabilities.jtbd: When I run the business, I want settings, users and permissions correct and safe.

importance.rationale: Occasional; governance/security-adjacent (indirect).

ui.note: settings, users, roles, config

agent.note: mast_users, mast_user_admin, mast_config, mast_routing, mast_tokens, mast_skills

test.note: lint-rbac (live-source gate fails the instant prod RBAC drifts), business-entity-feature-mode.test.js, guided-header-branch.test.js

valueAtRisk.name: Run a class / booking program (W10)
valueAtRisk.why: A whole second revenue line (money-critical) is invisible to the agent and has zero behavioral test. Highest importance Ă— lowest coverage in the matrix.

valueAtRisk.name: Resolve a return / refund (W7)
valueAtRisk.why: Money-OUT with a real double/over-refund adversarial risk, entirely untested; the agent can observe but likely not drive the refund CF.

valueAtRisk.name: Restock from low inventory / PO (W4)
valueAtRisk.why: Agent write power was just added (mast_procurement receive â†’ CF restock + cost-basis), but the POâ†’receiveâ†’restockâ†’cost-basis money path has NO regression net. New write power, zero automated guard.

valueAtRisk.name: Reprice on material cost change (W5)
valueAtRisk.why: Margin-critical; agent-strong but no drift/reprice regression and the human surface is the weakest of the Make flows â€” locks-respected correctness is unguarded.

valueAtRisk.name: Storefront/brand builder + campaign authoring
valueAtRisk.why: Revenue-enabling front-of-house, but BY DESIGN outside the dual task-success spine â€” the correct instrument is visual-regression + human review, which does not exist yet (theme-sanity.html rots). Flagged distinctly from the should-add-tool gaps.

valueAtRisk.name: Customer value metrics (W9)
valueAtRisk.why: The customer base and every value metric (LTV/wholesale/segment/lapse) are invisible to the agent â€” mast_contacts is a different thin collection. A clean, well-scoped tool would close it.

valueAtRisk.name: Fulfillment tail â€” pack/ship (W2)
valueAtRisk.why: Production is now agent-covered, but pack/ship has no MCP tool â€” the agent can't close the loop to 'delivered'. Lower priority (fulfillment is legitimately hands-on) but a real residual agent gap on a Critical flow.

recommendations.rec: Add a mast_classes / mast_book MCP tool
recommendations.rationale: Unblocks a whole revenue line for the agent. Highest importance Ă— lowest coverage. (Building it is a separate product decision; the matrix recommends, it does not build.)

recommendations.rec: Build a refund oracle with an over/double-refund collateral check; verify the agent can DRIVE the refund CF (not just observe)
recommendations.rationale: Money-out + adversarial; the AppWorld-style collateral check the spine flagged is the right shape.

recommendations.rec: Extend the money-canary class to a procurement/restock canary (POâ†’receiveâ†’on-hand+cost-basis to the cent)
recommendations.rationale: Pin the newly agent-writable PO money path before it drifts â€” same value-assertion pattern as money-canary.test.js.

recommendations.rec: Add a mast_customers MCP tool (LTV/wholesale/segment/spend), distinct from the thin mast_contacts
recommendations.rationale: Clean, well-scoped; surfaces the customer-value layer the agent is currently blind to.

recommendations.rec: Add a reprice regression (drift â†’ reprice â†’ locks respected) + close the UI parity gap
recommendations.rationale: Margin-critical capability with the weakest combined UI+test coverage among the Make flows.

recommendations.rec: Stand up a visual-regression layer (Playwright snapshot / Chromatic) for Web/Market
recommendations.rationale: The by-design gap; the correct instrument for creative/aesthetic surfaces â€” keep it separate from the task-success spine.

recommendations.rec: Make 'refresh the live MCP tool surface' a step of every matrix refresh
recommendations.rationale: W4 and W2 agent coverage drifted POSITIVE between 2026-06-20 and 2026-06-22 and the ledger went stale. Coverage is a moving target â€” re-verify against the live registry, never memory.

coverageRefresh.note: Positive drift surfaced by live re-verification 2026-06-22 â€” the matrix is MORE covered than the 2026-06-20 ledger claimed. Logged so the ledger is corrected, not just the gaps.

upgrades.from: agent â—‘ partial â€” 'PO write UI-only (no clear tool)'
upgrades.to: agent âś… full â€” mast_procurement full lifecycle incl. receiveâ†’CF-restock; mast_vendors; materials_cost_history

upgrades.from: agent âž– none â€” 'no production/pack MCP tools'
upgrades.to: agent âś… full (production) â€” mast_production job/line-item/request lifecycle (3 live jobs on golden-auric)

upgrades.from: agent returned gross/net with no caveat (F14)
upgrades.to: agent now returns marginReliable/cogsLineMissingCount/cogsMissing (F14 closed, mcp #141; verified live golden-auric)

rollup.headline: Of 4 Critical + 11 High capabilities, the sharpest value-at-risk cells are: Book/classes (agent+test blind, a whole revenue line), returns/refund (untested money-out), restock/PO (new agent write power, no regression net), and reprice (margin-critical, untested + weak UI). The agent's Make/Ship operability materially improved (W4/W2) â€” re-verify the surface each refresh.
